title: 502 Bad Gateway: Wrong Upstream Port
description:
## The Situation

Your team's API service was just deployed. The service runs on **port 8081** and is confirmed healthy. But every request through the load balancer returns `502 Bad Gateway`.

nginx is running. The upstream service is running. Something in the proxy configuration is wrong.

## Your Task

1. Diagnose why nginx returns 502
2. Find the misconfiguration in the nginx config file
3. Fix it and reload nginx

## Useful Commands

```bash
# Check if the upstream app is running
curl http://localhost:8081/health

# Test through nginx (should return 200, currently returns 502)
curl -v http://localhost/api/health

# View the nginx config
cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/api

# Test config syntax
nginx -t

# Reload after fixing
nginx -s reload
```
hints: nginx returns 502 when it can't reach the upstream server. The upstream app is running fine — something in the proxy config is wrong.
Compare the port in the nginx upstream block against the port the actual service listens on.
Edit /etc/nginx/sites-available/api. In the upstream block, change the port from 8080 to 8081. Then run: nginx -s reload
